@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8mv","title":"Add --strict flag for legacy checksum behavior","description":"# Task: Add --strict flag for legacy checksum behavior ## Context Part of EPIC: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7) ## What to Do Add --strict flag that restores the original behavior where ANY checksum mismatch aborts installation. ### Behavior - Without --strict (default): Use new recovery flow - With --strict: All tools treated as critical, any mismatch aborts ### Use Cases - Security-conscious users who want no exceptions - CI/CD environments where reproducibility matters - Auditing/compliance scenarios ## Acceptance Criteria - --strict flag parsed in argument handling - When set, all tools treated as critical - Help text documents the flag - Default behavior is recovery flow ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Argument parsing, pass flag to security functions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:39.830135-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:04:39.956587-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:04:39.956587-05:00","close_reason":"Added --strict flag to install.sh that sets ACFS_STRICT_MODE=true. When enabled, all tools are treated as CRITICAL and any checksum mismatch aborts installation. Updated header documentation.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8mv","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-v8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:33.579339-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8xx","title":"End-to-end test: acfs-update","description":"## What Comprehensive testing of acfs-update: 1. Test on fresh ACFS install (all tools present) 2. Test on partial install (some tools missing) 3. Test each category individually 4. Test --dry-run mode 5. Test --yes mode (non-interactive) 6. Test failure handling (simulate failures) 7. Test logging output ## Test Scenarios - Fresh VPS with full ACFS install - Existing VPS with previous ACFS version - Missing tools (should skip gracefully) - Network failures (should report and continue) - Permission issues (should report and continue) ## Considerations - Need a test VPS or Docker container - Some tests may need mocking (network failures) - Should test as both root and non-root user ## Success Criteria - [ ] All categories update correctly - [ ] Dry-run shows accurate preview - [ ] Failures handled gracefully - [ ] Logs are comprehensive and useful - [ ] No regressions in existing functionality","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:10.04603-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:38:12.190216-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:38:12.190216-05:00","close_reason":"Created comprehensive Docker-based E2E test (tests/vm/test_acfs_update.sh) covering: --help, --dry-run, --quiet, --yes modes; category filters (--agents-only, --shell-only, --no-apt); log file creation; exit codes; missing tool handling; version display. Test follows same pattern as test_install_ubuntu.sh. Shellcheck clean.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8xx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-csv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:35.23095-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","title":"Create report_skipped_tools() post-install summary","description":"# Task: Create report_skipped_tools() post-install summary ## Context Part of EPIC: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7) ## What to Do After installation completes, report any tools that were skipped: ### Output ``` ⚠ The following tools were skipped due to checksum mismatches: → ntm: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh → bv: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh You can install these manually after verifying they are safe. Or wait for ACFS to update checksums and run: acfs update --stack ``` ### Storage - SKIPPED_TOOLS array populated during install - Also saved to state.json for persistence ## Acceptance Criteria - Only shown if SKIPPED_TOOLS is non-empty - Shows tool name and installer URL - Provides manual install command - Mentions acfs update as alternative ## Files to Modify - install.sh: Add report at end of successful install","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:38.550459-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:42.459331-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:42.459331-05:00","close_reason":"Added comprehensive skipped tools reporting: record_skipped_tool() with reason and URL, report_skipped_tools() showing detailed summary with manual install commands and acfs update alternative, get_skipped_tools_json() for state persistence.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4jr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:33.367598-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","title":"TASK: Add 'Your First 5 Minutes' section to launch-onboarding","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz (Post-Installation Onboarding Experience) ## Summary Add a prominent, step-by-step "Your First 5 Minutes" walkthrough to the launch-onboarding page that guides users through their first Claude Code interaction. ## Rationale The UX audit identified the critical "now what?" gap: > After "Congratulations!", users see \`cc\` command but don't know: > - That they need to authenticate first > - What folder to be in > - What to actually ask Claude This task creates a hand-holding first experience. ## Content Specification ### Section: "Your First 5 Minutes" Place this BEFORE the "What you can do now" cards. \`\`\`tsx

Your First 5 Minutes

Let's make sure everything works with a quick test run.

{/* Step 1 */}
1

Create a project folder

{/* Step 2 */}
2

Authenticate Claude

A browser window will open. Log in with your Anthropic account, then return here.

{/* Step 3 */}

Start Claude Code

After authenticating, this shortcut launches Claude Code.

{/* Step 4 */}

Your first prompt

In the Claude prompt, type:

Create a simple Python script that prints "Hello from AI!" and run it
{/* Step 5 */}

Watch the magic!

Claude will:

  • ✓ Create a file called hello.py
  • ✓ Write the Python code
  • ✓ Run the script for you
  • ✓ Show "Hello from AI!" in the output

🎉 Congratulations! You just used AI to write and run code!

\`\`\` ### Visual Design - Large, numbered steps (1-5) - Each step has clear heading - Commands have copy buttons - Expected outcomes are listed - Celebration at the end ### Mobile Considerations - Stack vertically on mobile - Numbers should be smaller but still visible - Copy buttons must work on touch ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] "Your First 5 Minutes" section exists - [ ] 5 clear, numbered steps - [ ] Each step has command and explanation - [ ] Authentication step sets expectations (browser opens) - [ ] Expected Claude behavior listed - [ ] Celebration message at end - [ ] All commands have copy buttons - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## Dependencies - FEATURE: Wizard Password-First Flow Updates (ensures users reach this page) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:23.424815-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:23.424815-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.313779-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.499814-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","title":"TASK: Add 'Your First 5 Minutes' section to launch-onboarding","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz (Post-Installation Onboarding Experience) ## Summary Add a prominent, step-by-step "Your First 5 Minutes" walkthrough to the launch-onboarding page that guides users through their first Claude Code interaction. ## Rationale The UX audit identified the critical "now what?" gap: > After "Congratulations!", users see \`cc\` command but don't know: > - That they need to authenticate first > - What folder to be in > - What to actually ask Claude This task creates a hand-holding first experience. ## Content Specification ### Section: "Your First 5 Minutes" Place this BEFORE the "What you can do now" cards. \`\`\`tsx

Your First 5 Minutes

\`\`\` ### Visual Design - Large, numbered steps (1-5) - Each step has clear heading - Commands have copy buttons - Expected outcomes are listed - Celebration at the end ### Mobile Considerations - Stack vertically on mobile - Numbers should be smaller but still visible - Copy buttons must work on touch ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] "Your First 5 Minutes" section exists - [ ] 5 clear, numbered steps - [ ] Each step has command and explanation - [ ] Authentication step sets expectations (browser opens) - [ ] Expected Claude behavior listed - [ ] Celebration message at end - [ ] All commands have copy buttons - [ ] Mobile-friendly layout ## Dependencies - FEATURE: Wizard Password-First Flow Updates (ensures users reach this page) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:23.424815-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:43:18.529572-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:43:18.529572-05:00","close_reason":"Your First 5 Minutes section implemented by FuchsiaHill. 5 numbered steps with authentication guidance, example prompt, expected outcomes, and celebration message. All quality gates pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-trkz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.313779-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-96cx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:55.499814-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-998","title":"Fix web build: commit missing Jargon component/dictionary","description":"apps/web/app/page.tsx imports @/components/jargon and @/lib/jargon but these files are missing from git (currently present only as untracked files). This breaks build/type-check/lint in a clean clone. Track the files, fix any lint/type issues, and ensure reduced-motion and keyboard accessibility are respected.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T20:36:58.073784-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T20:44:58.191838-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T20:44:58.191838-05:00","close_reason":"Resolved on main: landing page + jargon component/dictionary now tracked and wired (see commit 1ef012c). Confirmed apps/web lint + type-check pass.","labels":["a11y","build","web"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","title":"TASK: Create /wizard/verify-key-connection page","description":"## Parent Feature agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t66c (Wizard Password-First Flow Updates) ## Summary Create a new wizard page that guides users to verify their SSH key-based connection works after the installer has set it up. This closes the loop on the password-first flow and proves the key installation succeeded. ## Rationale After the installer prompts for and installs the SSH key, users need to: 1. Disconnect from the password-based session 2. Reconnect using their SSH key 3. Verify it works without password This step is crucial because: - Confirms key was installed correctly - Teaches user the "correct" connection method - Provides troubleshooting if key doesn't work ## Page Content ### Header "Verify Key-Based Connection" ~1 min ### Main Content \`\`\`tsx // Step 1: Disconnect

Step 1: Disconnect

Type 'exit' and press Enter to close your current session:

Your terminal should return to your local computer prompt.

// Step 2: Reconnect with key

Step 2: Reconnect with your SSH key

Now connect using your SSH key instead of password:

// Success indicator
  • No password prompt appeared
  • You see: ubuntu@vps:~$
If you had to enter a password, something went wrong - see troubleshooting below. \`\`\` ### Troubleshooting Section Common issues and fixes: 1. **Still asks for password** - Key may not have been pasted correctly during installer - Re-run installer: it will prompt for key again 2. **Permission denied (publickey)** - Key file permissions might be wrong - Run: \`chmod 600 ~/.ssh/acfs_ed25519\` 3. **Connection refused** - VPS may have rebooted - Wait 1-2 minutes and try again ### SimplerGuide Content Explain: - Why we verify this step - What "key-based authentication" means - Why no password is good (more secure, more convenient) ## Technical Details ### File Location \`apps/web/app/wizard/verify-key-connection/page.tsx\` ### Step Number This becomes step 8 in the new flow (after run-installer). ### URL \`/wizard/verify-key-connection\` ### Navigation - Previous: run-installer (or status-check) - Next: accounts ### State Requirements - Needs vpsIP from localStorage (same as ssh-connect page) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] New page exists at /wizard/verify-key-connection - [ ] Shows exit command first - [ ] Shows key-based SSH command - [ ] Success criteria clearly stated - [ ] Troubleshooting for common key issues - [ ] SimplerGuide explains the "why" - [ ] Proper navigation (prev/next) - [ ] Step number updated in wizardSteps.ts ## Dependencies - TASK: Update /wizard/ssh-connect (page flow order) - TASK: Integrate SSH prompt into installer (key must be installed first) ## Files Modified - apps/web/app/wizard/verify-key-connection/page.tsx (new file) - apps/web/lib/wizardSteps.ts (add new step) - Related pages (update navigation)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:20:51.279962-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T19:40:27.19223-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T19:40:27.19223-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-hy7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:22.933443-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9ntj","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-egf5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T19:21:23.092203-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-9x2","title":"Wizard: Windows SSH key commands have broken backslashes","description":"apps/web/app/wizard/generate-ssh-key/page.tsx uses windowsCommand strings with single backslashes (e.g., /Users/jemanuel\.ssh\...), which are treated as JS escapes and drop the backslashes. Fix to use double-escaped backslashes so the copied command is valid on Windows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T14:03:29.105181-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T14:04:03.163713-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T14:04:03.163713-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed Windows command strings in generate-ssh-key step to properly escape backslashes (so copied commands use /Users/jemanuel\.ssh\... instead of dropping slashes)."}
